Use cases include: Plotting Laplacian Eigenmap coordinates .tdr.obj$graph$LE$embed Exploring relationships markers (e.g., CD3 vs CD4) Overlaying metadata 2D embedding Creating custom QC plots (e.g., library size vs mitochondrial %) Points plotted randomized order (controlled .seed) avoid systematic overplotting bias groups overlap. numeric .color.feature, applies diverging color scale (blue-white-red) centered .midpoint (defaults median). categorical features, generates discrete colors interpolating .cat.feature.color.

title: Scatter Plot with Feature Coloring — scatterPlot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After processing lm.cells <- setup.tdr.obj(.cells = .cells, .meta = .meta) |>   get.landmarks() |> get.graph()  # Plot PC1 vs PC2 colored by cluster scatterPlot(.x.feature = lm.cells$pca$embed[,"PC1"],             .y.feature = lm.cells$pca$embed[,"PC2"],             .color.feature = lm.cells$landmark.annot$clustering$ids,             .x.label = "PC1", .y.label = "PC2",             .color.label = "Cluster")  # Marker expression relationship scatterPlot(.x.feature = lm.cells$scaled.landmarks[,"CD4"],             .y.feature = lm.cells$scaled.landmarks[,"CD8A"],             .color.feature = .meta$Condition,             .x.label = "CD4", .y.label = "CD8A",             .color.label = "Condition")  # Laplacian Eigenmap coordinates scatterPlot(.x.feature = lm.cells$graph$LE$embed[,1],             .y.feature = lm.cells$graph$LE$embed[,2],             .color.feature = lm.cells$landmark.annot$clustering$ids,             .x.label = "LE1", .y.label = "LE2") } # }

title: Initialize tinydenseR object for landmark-based analysis — setup.tdr.obj
code: setup.tdr.obj(   .cells,   .meta,   .markers = NULL,   .harmony.var = NULL,   .assay.type = "cyto",   .celltype.vec = NULL,   .verbose = TRUE,   .seed = 123,   .prop.landmarks = 0.1,   .n.threads = if (is.hpc()) {      max(RhpcBLASctl::blas_get_num_procs(),     RhpcBLASctl::omp_get_num_procs(), RhpcBLASctl::omp_get_max_threads(), na.rm = TRUE)      } else {      parallel::detectCores(logical = TRUE)  } )  setup.lm.obj(...)

title: Initialize tinydenseR object for landmark-based analysis — setup.tdr.obj
text: .cells named list file paths (character strings) pointing RDS files, containing one expression matrix per sample. RNA: sparse matrix (dgCMatrix) genes rows cells columns. cytometry: matrix cells rows markers columns. List names become sample identifiers. .meta data.frame sample-level metadata. Rownames must match names .cells exactly. Used batch correction downstream modeling. .markers Character vector marker names use landmark identification. applicable .assay.type = "cyto". NULL, uses markers first sample. Ignored RNA data (uses HVG selection instead). .harmony.var Character vector column names .meta use Harmony batch correction. Supported "RNA" "cyto" assay types. cytometry, Harmony operates SVD embedding (centered, scaled) marker matrix. NULL, batch correction performed. .assay.type Character string: "cyto" cytometry (default) "RNA" scRNA-seq. Determines normalization strategy feature selection approach. .celltype.vec Optional named character vector mapping cell IDs cell type labels. .verbose Logical, whether print progress messages. Default TRUE. .seed Integer random seed ensure reproducibility. Default 123. .prop.landmarks Numeric 0 1 specifying proportion cells sample landmarks. Default 0.1 (10%). Total landmarks capped 5000 regardless. .n.threads Integer parallel processing. Default automatically detects maximum available threads (using BLAS settings HPC, detectCores() locally). ... Arguments passed setup.tdr.obj().

title: Initialize tinydenseR object for landmark-based analysis — setup.tdr.obj
text: list object initialized structure containing: $cells Input file paths $metadata Sample metadata added cell count columns $config Configuration parameters: $key Named vector mapping future landmarks samples $sampling Sampling parameters including n.cells, n.perSample $assay.type Assay type ("cyto" "RNA") $markers Marker list (cytometry ) $n.threads Number threads parallel processing $integration Integration/batch correction results: $harmony.var Batch variables (provided) $harmony.obj Symphony reference object (populated get.landmarks) Empty slots landmarks, scaled.landmarks, raw.landmarks, pca, graph, map, etc. - populated downstream functions

title: Initialize tinydenseR object for landmark-based analysis — setup.tdr.obj
text: function: Validates input data structure compatibility Calculates number landmarks sample per sample (max 5000 total) Creates "key" vector mapping landmarks samples Initializes empty slots downstream analyses (PCA, graph, mapping) Performs quality checks (warns sample sizes vary >10-fold) landmark sampling strategy aims proportional representation across samples capping total landmarks 5000 computational efficiency. Large samples capped prevent domination ensure adequate representation.

title: Initialize tinydenseR object for landmark-based analysis — setup.tdr.obj
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Prepare data (from README workflow) .meta <- get.meta(.obj = sim_trajectory, .sample.var = "Sample") .cells <- get.cells(.exprs = sim_trajectory,                      .meta = .meta,                     .sample.var = "Sample")  # Basic setup for RNA data tdr.obj <- setup.tdr.obj(   .cells = .cells,   .meta = .meta,   .assay.type = "RNA",   .prop.landmarks = 0.15 )  # Cytometry workflow with marker selection tdr.obj <- setup.tdr.obj(   .cells = .cells,   .meta = .meta,   .markers = c("CD3", "CD4", "CD8", "CD19"),   .assay.type = "cyto" )  # RNA workflow with batch correction tdr.obj <- setup.tdr.obj(   .cells = .cells,   .meta = .meta,   .harmony.var = "batch",   .assay.type = "RNA" ) } # }

title: tinydenseR 0.0.3.0001
text: tinydenseR clustering-independent framework sample-level modeling single-cell data. constructs landmark--sample fuzzy density matrix UMAP-derived cell–landmark connection strengths supports four downstream analysis modes within single analytical scaffold: Differential cell-state density analysis — landmark-level linear modeling density shifts across conditions pePC (partial-effect Principal Component projections) — supervised quantitative sample embedding isolates variation attributable specified effect plsD (graph-diffused, density contrast-aligned partial least squares Decomposition) — multivariate feature interpretation identifying expression programs aligned density contrasts Pseudobulk differential expression — manifold-informed, connection- strength-weighted pseudobulk aggregation design marker modes

title: tinydenseR 0.0.3.0001
text: data container formal S4 class 12 slots, providing structured representation landmark-based single-cell analysis objects. backward-compatible $ accessor preserves legacy syntax (obj$pbDE, obj$markerDE, etc.) internal code uses @-access.

title: tinydenseR 0.0.3.0001
text: analysis plot functions dispatch via S3 generics methods multiple input formats: RunTDR(): Unified entry point sets TDRObj supported container. GetTDR() / SetTDR(): Extract / embed TDRObj within container. S3 methods follow three-step pattern: extract → compute TDRObj → re-embed, functions work identically backend.

title: tinydenseR 0.0.3.0001
text: get.pbDE() supports two analysis modes: Pseudobulk aggregation uses connection-strength-weighted sums landmark neighborhoods rather hard cluster cell-type membership, yielding manifold-informed pseudobulk representation retaining samples unit inference.

what: plsD: graph-diffused partial least squares decomposition
title: tinydenseR 0.0.3.0001
text: get.plsD() identifies graph-smoothed expression programs maximally aligned fitted density contrast. Key properties: Sparse-implicit NIPALS PLS1 implementation (dense intermediates) Concordance-filtered raw loadings Post-hoc sign alignment density contrast Designed interpretation hypothesis generation, formal inference
